title: Push Notifications in React Native Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of Expo push tokens, permission flow, notification handlers, and server-side sending.

questions.question: Why does `registerForPushNotifications` return null on an iOS Simulator or Android Emulator?

options.text: The Expo SDK has a known bug that prevents token generation on simulators
Simulators and emulators do not have the hardware and OS-level push service integration required to receive push notifications — a physical device is required
FCM and APNs require a paid developer account to work on simulators
Simulators cannot connect to the internet, preventing token generation

questions.question: What is an Expo Push Token, and why must it be stored on your backend?

options.text: A temporary session token for authenticating Expo CLI commands
A unique identifier for a specific app installation on a specific device — stored on the backend so you can target that device when sending notifications from your server
An API key from Expo that grants access to their push notification delivery quota
A cryptographic signature proving the notification came from your verified server

questions.question: What is the correct handler to use when you want to navigate to a specific screen when a user TAPS a notification?

options.text: addNotificationReceivedListener — it fires when the notification appears
addNotificationResponseReceivedListener — it fires specifically when the user interacts with (taps) a notification
setNotificationHandler — it controls where to navigate on all notifications
addBackgroundNotificationListener — it fires when the app is backgrounded

questions.question: Your backend receives a `DeviceNotRegistered` error after attempting to send a push notification. What action should you take?

options.text: Retry the notification send 3 more times with exponential backoff
Remove the push token from your database — it means the user uninstalled the app and the token is no longer valid
Contact Expo support to re-register the device token
Switch from APNs to FCM for that device

questions.question: Why should you re-register for push notifications on every app launch rather than only at signup?

options.text: Expo push tokens expire after 24 hours for security reasons
The push token can change after an app reinstall, iOS update, OS reset, or device transfer — re-registering ensures your backend always has the current valid token
APNs requires re-registration every launch to prevent token staleness fees
The Expo SDK clears the previous token on every cold start
